response:
"Sec. 1  
[The equation involves a fourth degree polynomial and an absolute value function. To analyze the number of real solutions, it is helpful to consider the behavior of the left-hand side of the equation as a function of x. The term 1/3 x^4 grows rapidly as x moves away from zero in either the positive or negative direction, while the term 5|x| grows linearly. This suggests that the function has a minimum value at x = 0, where it equals 0, and increases as |x| increases. The right-hand side of the equation is a constant, 7, so we are looking for the number of points where the left-hand side equals 7.]  

Sec. 2  
[By analyzing the function f(x) = 1/3 x^4 + 5|x|, we can observe that it is an even function, meaning it is symmetric about the y-axis. Therefore, any solution for x > 0 will have a corresponding solution for x < 0. At x = 0, f(x) = 0, which is less than 7. As x increases, the value of f(x) increases, and since the function is continuous, it will cross the value 7 at some point. Due to the symmetry, there will be two solutions for x > 0 and two solutions for x < 0, resulting in a total of four real solutions to the equation.]"
